title: Executive, Staff Engagement and Wellness
description:
• Reputable Healthcare Organisation
  • Good opportunity for professional development
  • Competitive remuneration

As a Executive, Staff Engagement and Wellness, you will be responsible for the following duties:

  • Collaborate with internal stakeholders (HR teams, department heads) and external partners to understand organizational well-being goals.
  • Translate organizational strategies into actionable programs that promote employee well-being.
  • Implement initiatives aimed at building a resilient workforce, possibly including workshops, seminars, or ongoing training sessions.
  • Provide guidance on best practices for fostering a supportive and healthy work environment.
  • Conduct regular assessments and surveys to gauge employee well-being, burnout levels, and overall satisfaction.
  • Analyze survey results to identify trends and areas needing intervention.
  • Collaborate with relevant stakeholders to plan and execute interventions based on survey findings.
  • Plan and develop training materials focused on mental health literacy and awareness.
  • Execute campaigns to increase staff understanding of mental health issues and resources available.
  • Utilize data insights to monitor the effectiveness of well-being initiatives and adjust programs as needed.
  • Prepare reports and presentations for senior management to demonstrate the impact of well-being programs on employee health and productivity.
  • Provide support and intervention for individuals or groups facing emotional challenges.
  • Assist in planning and organizing hospital-level activities such as health workshops, sports events, and social gatherings.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to promote participation and foster a sense of community among staff.
  • Support the department's efforts to enhance connectivity through digital platforms.
    Oversee recruitment, orientation, and training of Welfare Officers (WOs)

Requirements

  • Bachelor Degree in Health Management & Promotion (Health Services Management etc) or related field of studies.
  • At least 2 year of relevant experiences in managing employee health and well-being is an advantage.

To apply, simply click on the ‘’apply’’ button in the job advertisement or alternatively, you can send in your resume via email:
Email Address: APPLY@ALLIEDSEARCH.COM.SG

We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

ALLIED SEARCH PTE. LTD.
EA LICENSE : 19C9777
